reasoning: Start by varying initial position to observe its effect on position.
Varying velocity to observe its effect on position.
Start by varying time to observe its effect on position.
Varying time to observe its effect on position, since it's the only variable not yet changed.
Varying initial position to observe its effect on position.
Varying velocity to observe its effect on position, since the change in position from initial position change was not significant.

The current position is directly proportional to the initial position, time, and velocity, and inversely proportional to the change in time, but since time hasn't changed, it must be directly proportional to time. The equation should be of the form position = a*initial_position + b*velocity + c*time.
